title: Cozy Moroccan Almond Sellou
description: A nutty and tender Moroccan dish featuring cumin and a deglaze, perfect for a cozy evening, this flexitarian-friendly sellou is a delicious and satisfying treat.
instructions: In a large skillet, toast the cumin seeds and coriander seeds over medium heat until fragrant.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced almonds, toasted cumin seeds, and toasted coriander seeds.
In a small bowl, whisk together the honey, orange blossom water, and rose water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until the almonds are well coated.
Add the melted butter to the almond mixture and stir until everything is well combined.
Spread the almond mixture out into a large sellou shape on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the sellou for 20 minutes, or until it is lightly golden brown.
Remove the sellou from the oven and let it cool completely.
Once the sellou is cool, use a sharp knife to cut it into diamond-shaped pieces.
Serve the sellou warm, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
